title: bassia sedoides var. taurica
intro: Bassia sedoides var. taurica, also known as Turkish sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb with small white flowers, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and skin conditions, ultimately providing benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Turkish sandwort can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms for therapeutic purposes.
